cb_step_1.title: Select Pelvic CT Volume
cb_step_1.simple: Choose the pelvic CT volume from the 'Input CT Volume' drop-down.
cb_step_1.detailed: The pelvic CT scan provides the 3D anatomical data needed for fracture segmentation and reduction planning. Selecting the correct volume allows the software to process the anatomy of interest.

cb_step_2.title: Segment Pelvis
cb_step_2.simple: Click the 'Run Step 1: Segment Pelvis' button.
cb_step_2.detailed: This step automatically segments the pelvic bone from the CT volume, creating a mask that will be used as a reference for fracture alignment and screw planning.

cb_step_3.title: Segment Fractures
cb_step_3.simple: Click the 'Run Step 2: Segment Fractures' button.
cb_step_3.detailed: This step identifies and isolates individual fracture fragments from the pelvic bone. Each fragment is separated for independent reduction.

cb_step_4.title: Exclude Fragments
cb_step_4.simple: Uncheck any fracture fragments you want to exclude from planning.
cb_step_4.detailed: Fragments that are not part of the surgical reduction (e.g., small or non-displaced pieces) can be excluded to simplify the view. Only fragments that need alignment should remain checked.

cb_step_5.title: Generate Template
cb_step_5.simple: Click the 'Run Step 3: Generate Template' button.
cb_step_5.detailed: This step creates a template based on the intact pelvic anatomy, serving as the target alignment for fracture reduction.

cb_step_6.title: Register & Reduce
cb_step_6.detailed: This step automatically registers the fracture fragments to the pelvis template, simulating anatomical reduction and reconstructing the pelvic ring.

cb_step_7.title: Manual Adjustments?
cb_step_7.simple: If further adjustments are needed, check the 'Manually adjust a fragment' box. Otherwise, skip to step 11.
cb_step_7.detailed: After automated reduction, you can fine-tune individual fragment positions. If the reduction is already satisfactory, proceed directly to screw planning.

cb_step_8.title: Select Fragment
cb_step_8.simple: In the 'Fragment' selection box, choose the fragment you want to adjust.
cb_step_8.detailed: Select the specific fracture fragment that requires manual repositioning to achieve an optimal anatomical fit.

cb_step_9.title: Adjust Fragment
cb_step_9.simple: Use the transform widget to reposition and rotate the selected fragment.
cb_step_9.detailed: Fine-tune the fragment's location and orientation to perfect alignment. Visual feedback in the 3D view confirms proximity to adjacent bone surfaces.

cb_step_10.title: Apply Adjustments
cb_step_10.detailed: Saves the manual adjustments and updates the reduction plan with the refined fragment position.

cb_step_11.title: Plan Screws
cb_step_11.simple: Click the 'Run Step 5: Plan Screws' button.
cb_step_11.detailed: This step automatically generates optimal screw trajectories for pelvic fracture fixation, considering bone corridors and safety margins.

cb_step_12.title: Edit Screw Trajectories?
cb_step_12.simple: If you need to modify the screw paths, check the 'Edit Screw trajectories' box. Otherwise, the planning is complete.
cb_step_12.detailed: Automatically planned screws may be adjusted to avoid critical structures or improve bone purchase. Selecting 'No' finalizes the plan.

cb_step_13.title: Adjust Screw Trajectories
cb_step_13.simple: Use the transform widget to reposition and rotate the screw trajectories.
cb_step_13.detailed: Fine-tune entry point, direction, and depth of each screw for optimal fixation. Visualize the paths relative to bone and fracture lines.

cb_step_14.title: Regenerate Screws
cb_step_14.simple: Click the 'Regenerate screws from edited lines' button.
cb_step_14.detailed: Updates the screw plan to reflect manually adjusted trajectories, ensuring accurate screw lengths and orientations for the final guide.

cb_step_15.title: Disable Edit Mode
cb_step_15.simple: Uncheck the 'Edit Screw trajectories' checkbox to exit edit mode.
cb_step_15.detailed: Finalizes screw planning. The plan is now ready for review and export.
